verdict: AI displacement risk: moderate, with potential for significant disruption in experimental and theoretical work.

reasoning_steps.title: High routine cognitive tasks
reasoning_steps.body: Physicists perform complex calculations and analyze data from research, which are tasks that current LLMs can already handle with high accuracy.

reasoning_steps.title: Significant AI capability gap
reasoning_steps.body: While physicists rely on mathematical models and theories, the development of new theories and laws requires human creativity and judgment, which is still challenging for current AI systems.

reasoning_steps.title: Limited regulatory moat
reasoning_steps.body: Theoretical physics is not heavily regulated, and while there are some guidelines, they do not provide a strong barrier against AI displacement.

adjacent_careers.name: Research Scientist
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Both physicists and research scientists rely on mathematical models and theories, but research scientists often work in more applied fields.

adjacent_careers.name: Theoretical Physicist
adjacent_careers.reasoning: While theoretical physicists focus on developing new theories and laws, their work is still closely tied to experimental physics.

recent_developments.headline: New AI-powered simulation tool for particle physics
recent_developments.impact: This development could potentially automate some tasks in particle physics, but it is unlikely to replace the need for human creativity and judgment in theoretical work.

recent_developments.headline: AlphaFold reshapes structural biology research
recent_developments.impact: Predicted protein structures are now a default starting point for experimental design.

quotes.quote: AlphaFold has predicted the structure of nearly every known protein. Tasks that took a PhD student years now take seconds. The bottleneck has moved to the experiments and the questions.
quotes.speaker: Demis Hassabis
quotes.title: CEO, Google DeepMind; Nobel laureate in Chemistry 2024

quotes.quote: AI is the most profound technology humanity is working on, more profound than fire or electricity. In science it is already changing what is knowable.
quotes.speaker: Sundar Pichai
quotes.title: CEO, Google / Alphabet

quotes.quote: I can imagine a world where AI agents run thousands of experiments in parallel, and the human scientist's role becomes one of asking the right question and interpreting the result.
quotes.speaker: Fei-Fei Li
quotes.title: Co-Director, Stanford Institute for Human-Centered AI
quotes.source: The Worlds I See / public lectures
